description:
You are acting as an “artifact-manager” in charge of curating a very small,
    local, *binary* repository under the user’s home directory.

    1.  A **staging** directory already exists:
            /home/user/temp/artifacts

        It contains three **empty** binary files that need to be moved into a
        proper repository structure:

            • toolA-1.0.bin
            • toolB-2.5.bin
            • utilC-0.9.bin

    2.  Your goal is to build the final repository tree exactly as below
        (categories are case-sensitive):

            /home/user/binary-repo/
            ├── tools/
            └── utils/

        • toolA-1.0.bin  →  tools  
        • toolB-2.5.bin  →  tools  
        • utilC-0.9.bin  →  utils  

    3.  After moving the binaries you must generate a **manifest file**
            /home/user/binary-repo/manifest.csv

        3.1  The *first line* must be the header **exactly**:

                filename,sha256,size,category

        3.2  Follow with one line per binary, **sorted alphabetically by the
             filename column** (toolA …, toolB …, utilC …).

        3.3  Column meanings (comma-separated, no spaces):
              • filename   – just the file’s base name (e.g. toolA-1.0.bin)
              • sha256     – lower-case hexadecimal digest of the file
              • size       – file size in bytes (all three files start out as 0)
              • category   – exactly “tools” or “utils” as assigned above

        3.4  A trailing newline after the last line **is required**.

    4.  Create a minimal **action log**
            /home/user/binary-repo/actions.log

        The file must contain these four lines *in this exact order*
        (no extra spaces, case-sensitive, each followed by a newline):

            move:/home/user/temp/artifacts/toolA-1.0.bin:/home/user/binary-repo/tools/toolA-1.0.bin
            move:/home/user/temp/artifacts/toolB-2.5.bin:/home/user/binary-repo/tools/toolB-2.5.bin
            move:/home/user/temp/artifacts/utilC-0.9.bin:/home/user/binary-repo/utils/utilC-0.9.bin
            manifest:generated

    5.  Summary of what the automated checker will verify:
        • All three binaries exist in their **new** category directories.
        • /home/user/binary-repo/manifest.csv exists and its contents match the
          format and order described above.
        • /home/user/binary-repo/actions.log exists and its four lines are
          *exactly* as shown in step 4 (including newlines).
        • The original files are **no longer** present in
          /home/user/temp/artifacts.

    6.  You are free to choose any commands you like, but a correct solution
        typically requires about 5–10 shell commands.

    7.  You do **not** need root privileges.  Work entirely inside /home/user.

    8.  When you are done, simply return the prompt; no additional narrative
        output is necessary.
